page: Amazon Web Services m8g.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top 10%, showing high read and write bandwidth for both small cached and large uncached blocks. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is average, as evidenced by middle-tier scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Additionally, prompt processing for small 135M parameter language models falls into the bottom 25% of benchmarks, though prompt processing for medium 7B models is strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8g.48xlarge leverages the Graviton arm64 architecture to deliver high multi-core compute density and memory bandwidth without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, which requires external network-attached storage, and the absence of GPU acceleration. This instance is suited for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and 192 dedicated arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and small-model prompt processing remain average to weak. The instance offers high resource density and multi-core efficiency for parallel workloads. It is suited for large-scale database deployments, Redis caching, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
